author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, Matt. xiii. 52, ‘Therefore every scribe who hath been made a disciple to the kingdom of Heaven,’ etc.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Matt. xiii. 52, ‘Therefore every scribe who hath been made a disciple to the kingdom of Heaven,’ etc.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The lesson of the Gospel reminds me to seek out, and to explain to you, Beloved, as the Lord shall give me power, who is “that Scribe instructed in the kingdom of God, who is “like unto an householder bringing out of his treasure things new and old.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For here the lesson ended.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “What are the new and old things of an instructed Scribe?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now it is well known who they were, whom the ancients, after the custom of our Scriptures, called Scribes, those, namely, who professed the knowledge of the Law.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For such were called Scribes among the Jewish people, not such as are so called now in the service of judges, or the custom of states.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For we must not enter school to no purpose, but we must know in what signification to take the words of Scripture; lest when anything is mentioned out of it, which is usually understood in another secular use of the term, the hearer mistake it, and by thinking of its customary meaning, understand not what he has heard.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Scribes then were they who professed the knowledge of the Law, and to them belonged both the keeping and the studying, as well as also the transcribing and the expounding, of the books of the Law.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Such were they whom our Lord Jesus Christ rebukes, because they have the keys of the kingdom of heaven, and “would neither enter in themselves, nor suffer others to enter in;” in these words finding fault with the Pharisees and Scribes, the teachers of the law of the Jews.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Of whom in another place He says, “Whatsoever they say, do, but do not ye after their works, for they say and do not.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why is it said to you, “For they say and do not?” but that there are some of whom what the Apostle says, is clearly exemplified, “Thou that preachest a man should not steal, dost thou steal?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou that sayest a man should not commit adultery, dost thou commit adultery?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou that abhorrest idols, dost thou commit sacrilege?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou that makest thy boast of the Law, through breaking the Law dishonourest thou God?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the name of God is blasphemed among the Gentiles through you.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is surely plain that the Lord speaks of these, “For they say and do not.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They then are Scribes, but not “instructed in the kingdom of God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Peradventure some of you may say, “And how can a bad man speak what is good, when it is written, in the words of the Lord Himself, ‘A good man out of the good treasure of his heart bringeth forth good things, and an evil man out of the evil treasure of his heart bringeth forth evil things.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye hypocrites, how can ye being evil speak good things?’”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In the one place He says, “How can ye being evil speak good things?” in the other He says, “What they say, do, but do ye not after their works.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For they say, and do not.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If “they say and do not,” they are evil; if they are evil, they cannot “speak good things;” how then are we to do what we hear from them, when we cannot hear from them what is good?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now take heed, Holy and Beloved, how this question may be solved.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whatever an evil man brings forth from himself, is evil; whatever an evil man brings forth out of his own heart, is evil; for there is the evil treasure.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But whatever a good man brings forth out of his heart, is good; for there is the good treasure.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whence then did those evil men bring forth good things?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Because they sat in Moses’ seat.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Had He not first said, “They sit in Moses’ seat;” He would never have enjoined that evil men should be heard.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what they brought forth out of the evil treasure of their own heart, was one thing; another what they gave utterance to out of the seat of Moses, the criers so to say of the judge.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What the crier says, will never be attributed to him if he speak in the presence of the judge.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What the crier says in his own house is one thing, what the crier says as hearing it from the judge is another.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For whether he will or no, the crier must proclaim the sentence of punishment even of his own friend.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And so whether he will or no, must he proclaim the sentence of the acquittal even of his own enemy.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Suppose him to speak from his heart; he acquits his friend, and punishes his enemy.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Suppose him to speak from the judge’s chair; he punishes his friend, and acquits his enemy.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So with the Scribes; suppose them to speak out of their own heart; thou wilt hear, “Let us eat and drink, for to-morrow we shall die.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Suppose them to speak from Moses’ seat; thou wilt hear, “Thou shalt not kill, Thou shalt not commit adultery, Thou shall not steal, Thou shall not bear false witness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Honour thy father and mother; thou shall love thy neighbour as thyself.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do then this which the official seat proclaims by the mouth of the Scribes; not that which their heart utters.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For so embracing both judgments of the Lord, thou wilt not be obedient in the one, and guilty of disobedience in the other; but wilt understand that both agree together, and wilt regard both that as true, “that a good man out of the good treasure of his heart bringeth forth good things, and an evil man out of the evil treasure bringeth forth evil things;” and that other also, that those Scribes did not speak good things out of the evil treasure of their heart, but that they were able to speak good things out of the treasure of Moses’ seat.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then those words of the Lord will not disturb you, when He says, “Every tree is known by his own fruit.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do men gather grapes of thorns, and figs of thistles?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Scribes and Pharisees of the Jews therefore were thorns and thistles, and notwithstanding, “what they say do, but do ye not after their works.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then the grape is gathered from thorns, and the fig from thistles, as He has given thee to understand according to the method I have just laid down.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For so sometimes in the vineyard’s thorny hedge, the vines get entangled, and clusters of grapes hang from the brambles.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hadst no sooner heard the name of thorns, than thou wert on the point of disregarding the grape.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But seek for the root of the thorns, and thou wilt see where to find it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Follow too the root of the hanging cluster, and thou wilt see where to find it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So understand that the one refers to the Pharisee’s heart, the other to Moses’ seat.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But why were they such as they were?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Because,” says St. Paul, “the vail is upon their heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And they do not see that the old things are passed away, and all things are become new.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hence it is that they were such, and all others who even now are like them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why are they old things?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because they have been a long while published.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because they relate to the kingdom of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How the vail then is taken away, the Apostle himself tells us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “But when thou shalt turn to the Lord, the vail shall be taken away.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then the Jew who does not turn to the Lord, does not carry on his mind’s eye to the end.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Just as at that time the children of Israel in this figure did not carry on the gaze of their eyes “to the end,” that is, to the face of Moses.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the shining face of Moses contained a figure of the truth; the vail was interposed because the children of Israel could not yet behold the glory of his countenance.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Which figure is done away.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For so said the Apostle; “which is done away.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why done away?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because when the emperor comes, the images of him are taken away.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The image is looked upon, when the emperor is not present; but where he is, whose image it is, there the image is removed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There were then images borne before Him, before that our Emperor the Lord Jesus Christ came.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When the images were taken away, the glory of the Emperor’s presence is seen.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore, “When any one turneth to the Lord, the vail is taken away.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the voice of Moses sounded through the vail, but the face of Moses was not seen.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And so now the voice of Christ sounds to the Jews by the voice of the old Scriptures: they hear their voice, but they see not the face of Him that speaketh.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Would they then that the vail should be taken away?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Let them turn to the Lord.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For then the old things are not taken away, but laid up in a treasury, that the Scribe may henceforth be “instructed in the kingdom of God, bringing forth out of his treasure” not “new things” only, nor “old things” only.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if he bring forth “new things” only or “old things” only; he is not “a scribe instructed in the kingdom of God, bringing forth out of his treasure things new and old.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If he say and do them not; he brings forth from the official seat, not from the treasure of his heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And (we speak the truth, Holy Brethren) what things are brought out of the old, are illustrated by the new.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore do “we turn to the Lord, that the vail may be taken away.”
